testPolicy.purpose: Approve an executable, deterministic proof plan for Workforce Domain, Application, focused ports, HTTP compatibility, tenant isolation, consistency, parity, and rollback without claiming missing target tests already pass.
testPolicy.contractAuthority: WF-PRE-05 through WF-PRE-12 artifacts are the only expected-behavior authority. A test cannot invent or normalize away a semantic difference.
testPolicy.currentEvidence: The existing Teacher Management backend scenario and smoke/hardening suites are legacy regression evidence only; no target Workforce module, adapter, Application contract suite, or port contract suite exists.
testPolicy.activationRule: Each planned command must exist, run from a clean checkout, and pass for its exact scope before the corresponding adapter, contract, route, cohort, or rollback step is eligible.
testPolicy.failureRule: Failed, skipped, flaky, quarantined, unknown, or adapter-omitted required cases are blocking. Expected output may change only through the governing contract decision.
testPolicy.routeEffect: WF-PRE-13 approves test specifications only. It enables zero target routes and authorizes no source module, adapter, schema, migration, or production change.
testPolicy.parityRule: Business DTO, semantic error, persisted state, authority, tenant, authorization, audit intent, ordering, and identifier mismatches have zero tolerance except the two explicitly governed target deltas.
testPolicy.normalizationRule: Normalize only generated timestamps/IDs supplied by deterministic fixtures and storage representation. Never normalize business fields, ordering, tenant identity, status, errors, privacy, or side effects.
testPolicy.executionEnvironment: Node.js 24, Asia/Tashkent timezone, isolated temporary SQLite, deterministic fake providers, fixed clock/ID sequence, and no network or production data.
testPolicy.evidenceRule: Every command publishes machine-readable case results, normalized diff, fixture/config hash, source commit, Node/database versions, duration, and terminal pass/fail.

deterministicControls.randomness: Forbidden unless explicitly seeded and recorded
deterministicControls.network: Forbidden; provider behavior uses deterministic fakes or local adapters
deterministicControls.database: A unique temporary SQLite database per test process; cleanup failure fails the suite
deterministicControls.concurrency: Explicit barriers/latches with bounded completion; sleeps and timing races are forbidden
deterministicControls.sensitiveData: Synthetic only; no production personal data, credentials, tokens, or secrets

fixtures.name: Two-tenant actor and Branch fixture
provides: Admin and Teacher actors per tenant
fixtures.cleanup: Dispose fake contexts and delete the process-owned temporary database

fixtures.name: Teacher lifecycle and Identity fixture
fixtures.cleanup: Assert no orphan Teacher/User/role/grant/session rows, then delete the process-owned database

fixtures.name: Profile provider projection fixture
fixtures.cleanup: Reset all fake-provider call logs and projection maps

fixtures.name: Working Hour boundary and concurrency fixture
fixtures.cleanup: Assert interval count/checksum, release barriers, and delete the process-owned database

fixtures.name: Provider fault and outcome fixture
fixtures.cleanup: Assert no unconsumed fault script and clear provider histories

fixtures.name: Legacy-target parity dataset
provides: logically identical legacy and target inputs
fixtures.cleanup: Persist diff artifact, assert both harnesses closed, and delete temporary state

fixtures.name: HTTP compatibility fixture
provides: shadow legacy and target dispatch hooks
fixtures.cleanup: Close local server, invalidate sessions, and remove temporary files

fixtures.name: Rollback and reconciliation fixture
fixtures.cleanup: Assert legacy authority, no target write route, and no unresolved test incident

suiteCatalog.name: Workforce Domain unit contracts
suiteCatalog.expected: Teacher lifecycle/value invariants and Working Hour validation/overlap/adjacency are deterministic and persistence-independent.

suiteCatalog.name: Workforce Application contract suite
suiteCatalog.expected: All 11 public contracts pass canonical success/error/auth/privacy/order/provider-failure cases through fakes with exact call ordering and prohibited effects.

suiteCatalog.name: Workforce owned SQLite port contracts
suiteCatalog.expected: All five owned ports and 12 methods satisfy DTO, tenant, ordering, conflict, rollback, driver-error, and concurrency contracts using only teachers and teacher_working_hours.

suiteCatalog.name: Provider anti-corruption port contracts
suiteCatalog.expected: All 11 provider ports and 18 methods preserve minimum DTOs, keyed completeness, semantic failures, tenant scope, timeout/unknown outcomes, and zero foreign-table access.

suiteCatalog.name: System clock and ID port contracts
suiteCatalog.expected: Clock and ID ports produce the fixed UTC instant and deterministic unique non-empty IDs without provider or Audit identity generation.

suiteCatalog.name: Workforce HTTP compatibility and privacy suite
suiteCatalog.expected: All ten frozen operations and 69 WFT behavior IDs match method/path/status/envelope/fields/order/privacy/error rules; common invalid-session/body/tenant cases pass.

suiteCatalog.name: Two-tenant isolation matrix
suiteCatalog.expected: All 11 public contracts and all 18 ports reject or hide same-ID foreign-tenant facts; no cross-tenant conflict, blocker, session, Audit intent, call, or DTO field appears.

suiteCatalog.name: Consistency and Audit fault-injection suite
suiteCatalog.expected: All 14 PRE-11 variants prove ordering, local rollback, legacy hold, no auto-retry, unknown/committed_unacknowledged outcomes, Audit acceptance, and prohibited compensation.

suiteCatalog.name: Legacy-target semantic parity suite
suiteCatalog.expected: All 11 public contracts produce zero unexplained semantic/effect mismatches; only WF-APP-DELTA-01 and WF-APP-DELTA-02 use separately asserted legacy-characterization/target-remediation expectations.

suiteCatalog.name: Route rollback and reconciliation contract suite
suiteCatalog.expected: Fallback selects frozen legacy authority, target writes remain disabled, known/unknown outcomes are classified, Audit reconciliation is duplicate-safe, and no foreign SQL or blind replay occurs.

crossCuttingCoverage.expected: All ten HTTP operations return frozen 401/403 and execute no target/legacy business mutation for invalid session or tenant.
Every body-consuming route rejects malformed/oversized JSON with 400/413 before use-case execution.
Every public contract and port proves two-tenant non-leak/non-mutation behavior.

parityPlan.passRule: Every required case passes with zero unexplained mismatch; both governed deltas pass their legacy characterization and target remediation assertions separately.

rollbackAndReconciliationCoverage.scenario: Target route not selected or fails before dispatch
rollbackAndReconciliationCoverage.expected: Known not_started result; no target/legacy mutation, no Audit intent, a later request may use the approved route policy.
rolled_back; exact pre-state checksum, no Audit intent, no automatic retry.

rollbackAndReconciliationCoverage.scenario: Business commit followed by explicit Audit failure
rollbackAndReconciliationCoverage.expected: committed_unacknowledged; technical failure, business state proven, no business replay, missing Audit handled only by approved reconciliation.

rollbackAndReconciliationCoverage.scenario: Business commit followed by ambiguous Audit acceptance
rollbackAndReconciliationCoverage.expected: committed_unacknowledged with unknown Audit substate; quarantine until Audit-owned evidence prevents duplicate append.
unknown; no fallback mutation or blind replay; public-query evidence and operator escalation required.
Frozen legacy authority is selected, target mutation remains disabled, tenant/HTTP behavior stays valid, and no synchronous dual write occurs.
Selector denies target invocation and executes only the governed frozen legacy route.

artifactContract.retention: Attach to the migration PR and retain under the CI evidence policy
artifactContract.redaction: No password, hash, cookie, token, personal data, or raw provider credential may enter an artifact

releaseAdmission.moduleReadinessEffect: WF-PRE-13 plan completeness passes, but Module Readiness remains Failed until WF-PRE-14 and WF-PRE-16; each extraction increment must implement and pass its named suite before activation.

risks.issue: Legacy end-to-end coverage can be mistaken for focused target contract evidence.
risks.treatment: Current evidence stays classified legacy-only; every target layer has a separate blocking suite and command.

risks.issue: Parity fixtures can bless target output or hide the two intentional contract deltas.
risks.treatment: Zero semantic tolerance, immutable predecessor expectations, and an exact two-item delta allowlist.

risks.issue: Audit timeout or post-commit failure can cause unsafe automatic replay and duplicate mutation.
risks.treatment: Fault suite proves outcome classification, no auto-retry, quarantine, and duplicate-safe reconciliation.

risks.issue: Tenant isolation may pass at HTTP while leaking through a port or composed provider projection.
risks.treatment: The two-tenant suite covers all 11 public contracts, all 18 ports, same-ID collisions, provider calls, Audit intents, and DTOs.

risks.issue: Timing-based concurrency tests can be flaky and fail to prove Working Hour overlap protection.
risks.treatment: Use explicit barriers with bounded completion; sleeps and probabilistic races are forbidden.

guards.rule: All 69 applicable PRE-06 behavior IDs and three cross-cutting IDs have exact suite coverage.
All 11 PRE-09 public contracts, 18 PRE-10 ports, 32 port methods, nine adapter groups, and 14 PRE-11 variants are covered.
Parity has zero semantic tolerance and exactly two governed delta IDs.
Every planned suite has owner, fixtures, exact files, exact command, expected result, implementation status, and activation gate.
Missing/skipped/flaky/quarantined/unknown cases block activation and cannot be converted to expected output without governance.
Audit/unknown outcomes never auto-retry or use foreign SQL reconciliation.
WF-PRE-13 claims zero implemented planned suites, enables zero routes, and does not authorize runtime/source/schema changes.

deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-14: Exact route increments, cohort, numeric stop/rollback thresholds, reconciliation commands, observation window, rehearsal, and operational owners
deferredDecisions.WF-PRE-16: Final Module Readiness decision after all preparation evidence is linked
deferredDecisions.WF-EXT-02+: Implement and pass each approved suite before its named extraction increment or route activation
